zh.lifeSummary: Sarah Anne Emery於1598年出生在英國艾可。她於1625年與Richard Curtice在英國英格蘭多瑟特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1628年11月1日在美國麻薩諸塞州艾可薩萊姆享年30歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sarah Anne Emery于1598年出生在英国艾可。她于1625年与Richard Curtice在英国英格兰多瑟特结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1628年11月1日在美国马萨诸塞州艾可薩萊姆享年30岁。

nameMeanings.name: Emery
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the ancient Germanic personal name Amalric, composed of the elements Amal (a dynastic name among the Goths) + rīk ‘realm, power’. The name was introduced into England from France by the Normans, developing into the Middle English personal name Amery, Emery (from Old French Amauri, Emaurri). Compare Amery , Amory , and 2 below.

French: from the Old French personal name Aimery, ancient Germanic Haimeric, from haim ‘home’+ rīc ‘power(ful)’. This may occasionally also be a derivation of the English surname (see 1 above). Compare Lemery .

variants.name: Emory
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Emery  . This form of the surname has now died out in Britain.

variants.name: Amery
meanings: from the ancient Germanic personal name Amalric, composed of the elements Amal (a dynastic name among the Goths) + rīk ‘realm, power’. The name was introduced into England from France by the Normans, developing into the Middle English personal name Amery, Emery (from Old French Amauri, Emaurri).
occasionally perhaps also from the Middle English, Old French personal name Aimery, ancient Germanic Haimeric, from haim ‘home’+ rīc ‘power(ful)’. Compare Emery .

variants.name: Amory
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Emery  , a cognate of 3 below.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Daumeray in Maine-et-Loire, France, introduced to England by the Normans. The initial D- was often regarded as a preposition and the name wrongly divided as De Aumari. This false preposition was later lost, hence Amori, Amory. The name was probably then confused with Amery .
French: from the Old French personal name Amauri, from ancient Germanic Amalric, composed of the elements Amal (a dynastic name among the Goths) + rīk ‘realm, power’.

variants.name: Embry
meanings: English: habitational name either from Emborough (Somerset), any of the three places called Hembury (Devon), or any of three place called Henbury (Gloucestershire, Cheshire, Dorset). Emborough is from Old English emn ‘flat-topped’ + beorg ‘mound or hill’, while Henbury in Gloucestershire comes from Old English hēah ‘high’ + burh (dative byrig) ‘fortified place’. Henbury in Chesire has an uncertain first element, possibly Old English hæmed ‘community’.
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Emery   1.

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

variants.name: Anni
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: Sarah Anne Emery was born in 1598, in Essex, England. She married Richard Curtice in 1625, in Dorset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died on 1 November 1628, in Salem, Essex, Massachusetts, United States, at the age of 30.

ja.lifeSummary: Sarah Anne Emeryは1598年にイギリスエセックスで生まれた。1625年にイギリスイギリスドールセトでRichard Curticeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1628年11月1日にアメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州エセックスサレムで30歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Anne Emery(은)는 1598년 영국 에세스에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1625년 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트에서 Richard Curtice(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1628년 11월 1일 미국 매사추세츠 주 에세스 살렘에서 30세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Sarah Anne Emery родилась в 1598 г. в местности эссекс, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Curtice в 1625 г. в местности дорсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла 1 ноября 1628 г. в местности сеилем, эссекс, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 30 лет.
